title: 10 Herbal Creams For Itchy Skin
intro_desc: People with psoriasis often experience itchy skin, which can be really uncomfortable and affect their daily lives. The itchy skin can be caused by the body's immune system attacking healthy skin cells, leading to inflammation and flaking skin. This can make it hard for people with psoriasis to sleep, work, or even enjoy simple activities. The itching can be so intense that it leads to scratching, which can cause more damage to the skin and make it harder to treat. This can create a cycle of discomfort and frustration, making it challenging for people with psoriasis to manage their symptoms and feel confident in their own skin.  When it comes to treating itchy skin in psoriasis patients, topical creams can be a helpful option. These creams are applied directly to the skin and contain soothing ingredients that can help calm inflammation and reduce itching. Some creams contain plant-based extracts that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to calm the skin and reduce the urge to scratch. By applying these creams regularly, people with psoriasis may be able to find some relief from itchy skin and improve their overall skin health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These constituents help to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling. The flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to have a potent antioxidant effect, which helps to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.  If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying a Calendula officinalis cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin until it's fully absorbed. You can also add a few drops of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to enhance the cream's penetration and moisturizing effects.  Taking action with Calendula officinalis cream may lead to a reduction in itching and inflammation, as well as an improvement in skin tone and texture. You may notice that the redness and swelling in the affected area start to subside, and the skin begins to feel smoother and more comfortable. In some cases, the itching may completely stop, or at least become less intense, allowing you to get a good night's sleep or go about your daily activities without discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contain compounds like histamine dihydrochloride, which is actually a byproduct of the plant's natural defense mechanism, and other anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce itchy skin by blocking the production of chemical signals that tell nerve endings to itch. The cream also contains other constituents like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm the skin and reduce redness.  If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying a thin layer of Urtica dioica cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into your skin until it's fully absorbed, and avoid applying it to broken or open wounds.  When you take action and apply the cream, you may start to feel a reduction in itchiness within 15-30 minutes, as the cream begins to block the chemical signals that trigger itching. Over time, the inflammation and redness may also start to subside, allowing your skin to heal. Some people may experience complete relief from itching within a few days, while others may need to continue using the cream for a longer period to see results.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains properties that can help relieve itchy skin. The main constituent, tea tree oil, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and soothe the skin. This can provide quick relief from itching and discomfort.  To use these creams effectively for itchy skin, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day, and gently massage it in. Be sure to wash your hands after applying the cream.  When you take action, you can expect the itching to subside within a few hours, and the redness to fade over the next 24-48 hours. The skin may feel softer and more comfortable to the touch. In some cases, repeated use can also help to prevent future episodes of itchy skin. However, if the itching persists or worsens, you should consult a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy skin. The cream also contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, that can help calm irritation and promote healing. When you apply Aloe barbadensis cream to itchy skin, the soothing properties of the aloe vera can help reduce inflammation and itching, making it easier to focus on everyday activities. To use Aloe barbadensis cream for itchy skin, gently massage a small amount of the cream into the affected area, ideally after a warm bath or shower to help the skin absorb the cream more effectively. Within a few minutes, you may start to feel a decrease in itching and inflammation, and with continued use, the redness and irritation may begin to fade.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains several properties that help with itchy skin. These properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds like thymol and borneol, which help reduce swelling and soothe irritation. Arnica montana creams also contain triterpene saponins, which have anti-allergic and antihistamine effects, reducing itching and redness. When you apply Arnica montana cream to itchy skin, follow these steps: gently wash the area with soap and water, then pat it dry and apply a thin layer of cream. Gently massage the cream into the skin, following the product instructions for the recommended amount. As the cream absorbs into the skin, you may start to feel a cooling sensation and notice a reduction in itching and inflammation. Within a few days, you may see a decrease in redness and a reduction in the severity of itching. In some cases, you may also notice an improvement in skin texture and a reduction in scarring. However, if your itching persists or worsens, you should consult a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains compounds like allantoin, mucilage, and flavonoids, which make it a popular remedy for itchy skin. Allantoin has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the urge to scratch. Mucilage, a thick, protective coating, helps to lock in moisture and shield the skin from further irritation. Meanwhile, flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess potent antioxidant properties that combat free radicals and promote healthy skin.  If you're experiencing itchy skin, consider applying a Symphytum officinale cream directly to the affected area. Gently massage a small amount into the skin until it's fully absorbed. You can also add it to a warm bath for a more intense treatment.  When using Symphytum officinale cream, you can expect to experience a decrease in itching and redness within a few hours. The cream may also help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of damaged skin. Some users report seeing improvements in skin texture and tone over time, but this can vary depending on the individual and the underlying cause of the itchiness. As with any new skincare product, it's essential to follow the manufacturer's instructions and perform a patch test before using it extensively.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and itching in the skin by blocking the production of substances that cause itching. When you apply Glycyrrhiza glabra creams to itchy skin, the glycyrrhizin and flavonoids penetrate deep into the skin, calming and soothing the affected area.  To use Glycyrrhiza glabra creams for itchy skin, apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Make sure to wash your hands before and after applying the cream to avoid transferring the cream to other parts of your body or spreading the itch to others.  When you start using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, you may notice a decrease in itching and inflammation within a few hours. The skin may feel cooler and look less red, and the itching may become less intense. You may also notice an improvement in your skin's overall appearance and feel, with a reduction in redness and dryness. However, if your itchy skin persists or worsens, you should consult a doctor for further advice and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that help soothe itchy skin. These anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while also calming the skin. The gel in Aloe vera creams also has vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and zinc, which help protect and moisturize the skin.  If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying Aloe vera cream directly to the affected area. Gently massage it in until it's fully absorbed, and you may start to feel relief within a few minutes.  As the cream works to calm and soothe your skin, you can expect your itchiness to subside, and the redness to fade. Your skin may feel cooler and more comfortable, and you might even notice a decrease in the frequency of your itch attacks. Some users also report that Aloe vera creams can help reduce the appearance of scarring and hyperpigmentation, leaving their skin looking smoother and more even-toned.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains a combination of properties that help to soothe and calm itchy skin. It's rich in alkaloids, like cichoric acid and echinacoside, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects that can reduce redness and swelling. The cream also contains flavonoids and glycosides, which have antioxidant properties that can help to repair the skin and reduce itchiness. When you apply Echinacea purpurea cream to itchy skin, it can help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further irritation. To get the most out of Echinacea purpurea cream, apply it directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Gently massage the cream into the skin, making sure to cover the entire area. You can also use it in conjunction with other treatments, like cool compresses or oatmeal baths, to help relieve itchiness. It's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea cream is not a substitute for medical treatment, so if your itchy skin persists or worsens, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. If you follow these steps and apply Echinacea purpurea cream regularly, you may start to notice improvements in your itchy skin. The inflammation and redness may decrease, and the itchiness may become less severe. You may also experience a reduction in the frequency and duration of itchy episodes. However, keep in mind that everyone's skin is different, and it may take some time to see noticeable results. Be patient, and don't hesitate to seek medical attention if your symptoms persist or worsen.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains properties that can help with itchy skin due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic constituents, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have soothing effects on the skin. These compounds work by reducing redness and swelling, thereby providing relief from itching sensations.  When using Lavandula angustifolia creams for itchy skin, it's recommended to apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day, gently massaging it in until fully absorbed. Avoid applying too much cream, as this can cause more irritation.  Applying Lavandula angustifolia creams according to the recommended guidelines may help reduce the intensity and frequency of itchy skin episodes, promote faster healing of skin irritations, and leave the skin feeling smoother and more relaxed.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for itchy skin

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe itchy skin due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Certain herbs like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have been known to calm irritated skin and reduce itching. These teas can be consumed hot or cold, and some people find them to be a natural remedy for relieving itchiness.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas daily for itchy skin can be safe, but it's essential to choose teas with ingredients that are known to help with skin issues. Look for teas with soothing herbs like chamomile, calendula, or licorice root. Some herbal teas may interact with medications or have side effects, so be sure to check the ingredients and follow the recommended dosage.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with itchy skin, but it depends on the type of tea and how sensitive you are. Some people notice relief within a few hours or days after drinking tea like calendula or oat straw, while others might feel better after a week or two. It's different for everyone.
faq_time: For itchy skin, try drinking herbal tea in the evening. This is because many herbal teas, like chamomile and calendula, can help you relax and sleep better. When you're relaxed, your skin can heal faster. Drinking it in the evening may also help you get a good night's sleep, which is essential for skin health.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for itchy skin. This is because some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can speed up how your body processes other drugs. Other herbs, like ginkgo biloba, can slow it down. This means your medication might not work as well or could have stronger side effects.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and dealing with itchy skin, some herbal teas might help soothe the itch. However, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like pennyroyal and sage, can stimulate the uterus and cause problems. Others, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe, but it's still crucial to check their ingredients and brewing instructions carefully before consuming them.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and have itchy skin, you might consider herbal teas for relief. However, be cautious with what you drink. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe, but others like goldenseal and blue cohosh may not be suitable. Always check the ingredients and potential interactions.
title_2: Psoriasis Patients' 10 Favorite Herbal Creams for Itchy Skin
